message:
feat: LikeConstFast/CountIndexEqRun opcodes, sorter top-N, memchr LIKE, rightmost-hint insert, function caching, and aggregate codegen overhaul

A broad set of performance and correctness improvements across 7 crates
in the FrankenSQLite engine (~12K lines changed).

## New VDBE Opcodes (fsqlite-types/opcode.rs)

LikeConstFast (192) — Evaluate a literal-pattern LIKE fast path directly
against a register, bypassing the generic Function dispatch and LIKE
recursion. The codegen emits this when the LIKE pattern is a string
constant that matches one of the five fast-path shapes (MatchAll, Exact,
Prefix, Suffix, Contains).

CountIndexEqRun (193) — Count a run of equal first-column index keys by
advancing the cursor, used for optimized COUNT with indexed IN subqueries
where the count can be computed by scanning the index equality prefix.

## LIKE Fast Path Refactoring (fsqlite-types/value.rs)

The internal LikeFastPath enum is replaced with a public
SqlLikeFastPathKind enum with opcode_tag()/from_opcode_tag() methods for
VDBE serialization. The classify and match functions are now public
(classify_sql_like_fast_path, sql_like_fast_path_matches) so the codegen
and engine can use them directly.

ascii_ci_contains() is accelerated with memchr/memchr2 for the
first-character scan, replacing the byte-by-byte ASCII fold loop. The
memchr crate is added as a dependency of fsqlite-types.

## Sorter Top-N Optimization (fsqlite-vdbe/engine.rs)

SorterCursor gains a top_n_limit field: when ORDER BY is paired with a
simple LIMIT, only the best N rows are kept during insertion. New rows
are compared against the worst row in the buffer; if the new row is
better, it replaces the worst. This avoids sorting the full input when
the tail can never survive to the final output. The find_worst_row_index
helper uses the existing sort key comparison infrastructure.

## MemTable Improvements (fsqlite-vdbe/engine.rs)

- Append-path optimization in insert(): when the new rowid is greater
  than all existing rowids, push directly instead of binary searching
- row_values_by_rowid(): direct value lookup by rowid
- count_rowid_range(lower, upper): count rows in a half-open rowid range
  using binary search on both bounds
- iter_rows_in_rowid_range(): iterate rows in a rowid range

## Rightmost-Hint Insert (fsqlite-btree/cursor.rs)

table_insert_rightmost_hint() tries the rightmost-leaf append path for
callers that expect monotonically increasing rowids (e.g., autoincrement
INSERT loops). If the rowid is not greater than the current last, it
falls back to a full seek. This avoids the cursor.last() + seek overhead
for the common case.

## Function Resolution Caching (fsqlite-vdbe/engine.rs)

VdbeEngine gains scalar_function_cache and aggregate_function_cache
HashMaps keyed by opcode address. Functions are resolved from the
registry once on first execution and cached for subsequent invocations
of the same opcode, eliminating repeated Arc clone + registry lookup.

## Aggregate & GROUP BY Codegen Overhaul (fsqlite-vdbe/codegen.rs)

Major refactoring of aggregate function code generation (~1000 lines
replaced with ~2000 lines). Key improvements:
- COUNT(*) with indexed IN scan now handles variable-length equality
  prefix runs via CountIndexEqRun opcode
- Scalar subquery improvements for correlated and uncorrelated cases
- EXISTS rowid probe extraction for optimized semi-join patterns
- LikeConstFast emission for constant LIKE patterns in WHERE clauses
- Sorter top-N integration with codegen LIMIT analysis
- Expanded test coverage for aggregate GROUP BY ordering, HAVING,
  mixed aggregates, and edge cases

## Connection & Prepared Statement (fsqlite-core/connection.rs)

- Hot-path profiling counters for new fast paths
- PreparedDirectSimpleInsertExpr compilation improvements
- Direct simple INSERT fast path separation tests
- Read-only connection datetime builtin support (compat_tests.rs)

## Benchmarks & Baselines (fsqlite-e2e/)

- read_heavy_bench: expanded with LIKE pattern benchmarks, indexed
  COUNT variants, and GROUP BY scenarios
- large_txn_bench: additional insert pattern benchmarks
- comprehensive_bench: refactored benchmark runner output
- bd_1lsfu_1_operation_baselines: 528 lines of new baseline tests
  for operation correctness verification
- perf_runner: additional timing instrumentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

             autoincrement_sequence_fast_path_updates: 1,

+            direct_indexed_equality_query_hits: 0,
+            direct_rowid_range_query_hits: 0,
+            direct_count_star_query_row_hits: 0,
+            direct_rowid_lookup_query_row_hits: 0,
+            direct_count_star_rowid_range_query_row_hits: 0,
+            direct_count_indexed_rowid_probe_query_row_hits: 0,
             btree_copy_kernels: BtreeCopyProfileSnapshot {
                 local_payload_copy_calls: 2,
                 local_payload_copy_bytes: 96,
